root_text.an5.131:1.1: “Pañcahi, bhikkhave, aṅgehi samannāgato rājā cakkavattī dhammeneva cakkaṁ vatteti;
root_text.an5.131:1.2: taṁ hoti cakkaṁ appaṭivattiyaṁ kenaci manussabhūtena paccatthikena pāṇinā.
root_text.an5.131:2.2: Idha, bhikkhave, rājā cakkavattī atthaññū ca hoti, dhammaññū ca, mattaññū ca, kālaññū ca, parisaññū ca.
root_text.an5.131:2.3: Imehi kho, bhikkhave, pañcahi aṅgehi samannāgato rājā cakkavattī dhammeneva cakkaṁ pavatteti;
root_text.an5.131:2.4: taṁ hoti cakkaṁ appaṭivattiyaṁ kenaci manussabhūtena paccatthikena pāṇinā.
root_text.an5.131:3.1: Evamevaṁ kho, bhikkhave, pañcahi dhammehi samannāgato tathāgato arahaṁ sammāsambuddho dhammeneva anuttaraṁ dhammacakkaṁ pavatteti;
root_text.an5.131:3.2: taṁ hoti cakkaṁ appaṭivattiyaṁ samaṇena vā brāhmaṇena vā devena vā mārena vā brahmunā vā kenaci vā lokasmiṁ.
root_text.an5.131:4.2: Idha, bhikkhave, tathāgato arahaṁ sammāsambuddho atthaññū, dhammaññū, mattaññū, kālaññū, parisaññū.
root_text.an5.131:4.3: Imehi kho, bhikkhave, pañcahi dhammehi samannāgato tathāgato arahaṁ sammāsambuddho dhammeneva anuttaraṁ dhammacakkaṁ pavatteti;
root_text.an5.131:4.4: taṁ hoti dhammacakkaṁ appaṭivattiyaṁ samaṇena vā brāhmaṇena vā devena vā mārena vā brahmunā vā kenaci vā lokasmin”ti.

translation_text.an5.131:1.1: “Mendicants, possessing five factors a wheel-turning monarch wields power only in a principled manner.
translation_text.an5.131:1.2: And this power cannot be undermined by any human enemy.
translation_text.an5.131:2.2: A wheel-turning monarch knows what is right, knows principle, knows moderation, knows the right time, and knows the assembly.
translation_text.an5.131:2.3: A wheel-turning monarch who possesses these five factors wields power only in a principled manner.
translation_text.an5.131:2.4: And this power cannot be undermined by any human enemy.
translation_text.an5.131:3.1: In the same way, possessing five qualities a Realized One, a perfected one, a fully awakened Buddha rolls forth the supreme Wheel of Dhamma only in a principled manner.
translation_text.an5.131:3.2: And that wheel cannot be rolled back by any ascetic or brahmin or god or Māra or divinity or by anyone in the world.
translation_text.an5.131:4.2: A Realized One knows what is right, knows principle, knows moderation, knows the right time, and knows the assembly.
translation_text.an5.131:4.3: Possessing these five qualities a Realized One, a perfected one, a fully awakened Buddha rolls forth the supreme Wheel of Dhamma only in a principled manner.
translation_text.an5.131:4.4: And that wheel cannot be rolled back by any ascetic or brahmin or god or Māra or divinity or by anyone in the world.”
